fields.title: Fix subagent model resolution for models not in local registry
fields.description:
## Problem

Subagents fail with 'No API key found' when their configured models exist at the provider level but aren't in the local ModelRegistry built-in list. The main pi CLI handles these models fine.

## Root cause

`resolveModelPattern()` in `extensions/tau/src/agent/worker.ts:108` only searches `modelRegistry.getAll()` (built-in + models.json). When a model like `google-antigravity/gemini-3.1-pro-high` isn't in that list, it returns `undefined`. The session is then created without an explicit model, and `findInitialModel` picks whatever has auth (e.g. bedrock), which then fails or uses the wrong provider entirely.

The main pi CLI doesn't have this problem because it constructs the model object directly from provider/id when the user passes `--provider`/`--model` flags, bypassing registry lookup.

## What needs to change

When `resolveModelPattern()` can't find a model in the registry but the pattern is a fully-qualified `provider/model-id`, construct a Model object directly instead of returning undefined. The ModelRegistry has a method or the model can be constructed -- check how the main CLI does it (likely `modelRegistry.find()` or direct construction).

Alternatively, fix `createSessionForModelAsync` to handle the case where `resolveModelPattern` returns undefined for a fully-qualified pattern by constructing the model directly.

## How to test

1. Ensure `~/.pi/agent/agents/oracle.md` has `google-antigravity/gemini-3.1-pro-high` (a model NOT in local registry but valid at provider level).

2. Verify the model works directly as main agent:
```bash
pi --mode json --provider google-antigravity --model gemini-3.1-pro-high -p "it's a harness test, say hi"
```
This WORKS -- proves the model+auth are valid.

3. Verify subagent fails (the bug):
```bash
pi --mode json --provider moonshot --model kimi-for-coding -p "it's a harness test, say hi to oracle and check if he responded"
```
Oracle subagent fails with 'No API key found for google-antigravity' because resolveModelPattern returned undefined.

4. After fix, command #3 should work -- oracle should respond.

## Key files

- `extensions/tau/src/agent/worker.ts:108` -- `resolveModelPattern()` function
- `extensions/tau/src/agent/worker.ts:667` -- `createSessionForModel()` and `createSessionForModelAsync()` where resolvedModel is used
- Check how main CLI constructs Model objects from provider+id: `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/sdk.js` around `findInitialModel`

## Already verified

- `~/.pi/agent/auth.json` has valid OAuth tokens for google-antigravity and openai-codex
- `new AuthStorage().getApiKey('google-antigravity')` returns a key (auth works)
- `modelRegistry.getAll()` does NOT contain gemini-3.1-pro-high (only gemini-3-pro-high)
- `modelRegistry.find('openai-codex', 'gpt-5.2')` DOES work (that model IS in registry)
- The error 'No API key found' is misleading -- the real issue is model not found in registry

Run `npm run gate` in `extensions/tau/` when done.
fields.close_reason: Reuse parent modelRegistry/authStorage for worker/subagent spawns
